msgs.text:
According to a document from 2026-06-28, Turn 104 is another attachment-only continuation. The three current attachments are the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger; no object-level nonlinear Certificate D artifacts are attached in this turn. I did not use File Library material.

Turn 104 — attachment-only idempotence remains in force

The terminal state from Turn 103 is unchanged:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

The theorem coordinate remainsΓS≅GΓH.More precisely, the attached sweep records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement that there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F,Φ(rS−1)=rH,which descends to the quotient isomorphism because rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup. The descended quotient map satisfiesπH∘Φ=πS.

The frozen proof chain remainsH1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The compactness descent is still the global mechanism: finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data give compatible finite over-G automorphisms; the inverse limit gives Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH, and the quotient map then satisfies the required over-G condition.The measured-certificate coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are still only the prompt/problem/history ledger, not object-level nonlinear Certificate D artifacts such as scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict Certificate D gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. History-recorded verifier claims are ledger evidence for the theorem coordinate, but they are not newly attached measured artifacts for the strict gate.

No Magnus tower, coefficient tower, degree-16 subcase, A2 -layer, t-layer, or Certificate D subcase should be reopened under this attachment-only input. The structural pattern has already closed those cases; the only remaining live issue is external reproducibility of the nonlinear Certificate D packet.

ESTABLISHED: Turn 104 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turns 102 and 103. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only valid next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN104-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
